title: Smoky Tender Moroccan Lamb Tagine
description: A midnight-craving, smoky lamb tagine with tahini richness, Whole30-friendly and bursting with aromatic spices.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a heavy‑bottomed tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the lamb cubes in a single layer and sear until browned on all sides. Remove and set aside.
In the same pot, add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic, smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, cinnamon, harissa paste, smoked sea salt, and black pepper; cook for 1 minute so the spices bloom.
Return the lamb to the pot, then pour in the chicken broth, water, sliced apricots, and chopped figs. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and let the tagine cook for 90–120 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the lamb is fall‑apart tender.
Stir in the tahini and fresh lemon juice, adjusting seasoning with more smoked sea salt if needed.
Remove from heat, then fold in chopped cilantro and parsley for freshness.
Serve the tagine hot, optionally with a side of cauliflower rice or fresh flatbread if not Whole30.
Enjoy the smoky, aromatic flavors that satisfy a midnight craving.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
